alert: Security: spike in unauthorized API responses containing PII; audit_logger firing data-exfiltration rules. auth_service error budget burning; user_service seeing abnormal egress patterns.

api_gateway: jwt_validation_failures elevated; upstream auth_service returning 502/SSL handshake failure
auth_service: CRITICAL: server certificate EXPIRED for auth_service internal TLS (notAfter in the past)
openssl: certificate verify failed: certificate has expired
SSL_ERROR_SSL: session renegotiation failing; clients falling back to degraded cipher negotiation
connection_pool_saturation waiting on stalled TLS handshakes cpu=93%
user_service: unauthorized_access: user profile endpoint returning 200 with foreign tenant_id (policy violation)
data_exfiltration_heuristic: large JSON payloads to unusual client fingerprints
warning: authorization bypass suspected — auth tokens validated against failing auth_service
audit_logger: SIEM: correlation unauthorized_access + ssl_errors auth_service within 15m window

ground_truth.difficulty_rationale: Medium-hard: SSL/TLS expiry on auth appears as scattered 502s and auth errors; PII leakage in user_service misdirects toward app bugs until logs show certificate expiry.
teaching_points: Correlate SSL/certificate errors on auth with downstream unauthorized data access
Audit and security alerts may surface the blast radius before a single service metric tells the full story
